The tests check these same paths and patterns. The cargo check/clippy/test/fmt commands are standard CI checks implied by 'cargo check, clippy, tests, fmt must all pass'.

This describes WHAT to change and HOW, leaving minimal reasoning for the agent. However, it doesn't specify exact line numbers or exact replacement code, so it's borderline. solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests check for structural properties (field removed, getter removed, ancestor iteration pattern, use of is_range_in_type_checking_block) but don't assert on exact line positions or gold-specific variable names. Alternative implementations using different iteration patterns (parent chain, loop, for, .any()) would all pass test_type_checking_uses_ancestor_iteration since it checks for any of those patterns.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests include cargo check, cargo clippy, cargo test --lib, and cargo fmt which all execute the code/compiler. While some tests (test_scope_field_removed, test_scope_getter_removed, etc.) do text inspection, they are complemented by the compilation and test execution tests that verify actual behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_scope_field_removed, test_scope_getter_removed, test_type_checking_uses_ancestor_iteration, test_function_callers_updated, test_overloaded_function_callers_updated) would fail on the original code since the field, getter, and direct calls exist. A stub implementation (e.g., empty function body) would fail test_is_in_type_checking_block_not_stub and cargo test --lib. The combination ensures real implementation is needed.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors specified in instruction.md are tested: field removal (test_scope_field_removed), getter removal (test_scope_getter_removed), ancestor-based derivation (test_type_checking_uses_ancestor_iteration), caller updates (test_function_callers_updated, test_overloaded_function_callers_updated), and continued correct operation (test_cargo_check, test_cargo_test_lib, test_cargo_clippy, test_cargo_fmt_check).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived), descriptive function names, docstrings explaining what each test verifies, and annotations like [static], [pr_diff], [repo_ci], [agent_config].
